debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G : Graph α β
M : Set β
P : WList α β
hM : @Graph.IsMatching α β G M
h : @Graph.IsAugPath α β M G hM P
⊢ V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet)

failed_attempts:
intro v hv
  have h₁ : v ∈ V(G, M) := hv
  have h₂ : v ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
    have h₃ : v ∈ V(G) := by
      have h₄ : v ∈ V(G, M) := hv
      exact h₄
    have h₅ : v ∈ V(G, M) ∪ V(G, E(P)) := by
      exact Or.inl h₁
    have h₆ : v ∈ V(G, M) ∪ V(G, E(P)) := h₅
    have h₇ : (v ∈ V(G, M) ∪ V(G, E(P))) → v ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
      intro h₈
      simp_all [V_symmDiff]
      <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          by_cases h₉ : v ∈ V(G, E(P))
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_union]) <;>
          (try aesop)
        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          by_cases h₉ : v ∈ V(G, M)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Set.mem_union]) <;>
          (try aesop)
        })
    exact h₇ h₆
  exact h₂

have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
    intro x hx
    have h₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx
    have h₃ : x ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
      have h₄ : x ∈ V(G, M) → x ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
        intro hx'
        have h₅ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx'
        -- Since x is in V(G, M), we need to show it's also in V(G, M ∆ E(P))
        -- This is true because M is a subset of M ∆ E(P)
        have h₆ : M ⊆ M ∆ E(P) := by
          intro e he
          by_cases h₇ : e ∈ P.edgeSet
          · -- If e ∈ P.edgeSet, then e ∈ M ∆ E(P) because e is in both M and P.edgeSet
            have h₈ : e ∈ M := he
            have h₉ : e ∈ P.edgeSet := h₇
            simp_all [Set.mem_symmDiff]
            <;> tauto
          · -- If e ∉ P.edgeSet, then e ∈ M ∆ E(P) because e is in M and not in P.edgeSet
            have h₈ : e ∈ M := he
            have h₉ : e ∉ P.edgeSet := h₇
            simp_all [Set.mem_symmDiff]
            <;> tauto
        -- Since x is in V(G, M) and M ⊆ M ∆ E(P), x is in V(G, M ∆ E(P))
        have h₇ : x ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
          obtain ⟨e, he⟩ := h₅
          have h₈ : e ∈ M ∆ E(P) := h₆ he.2
          refine' ⟨e, _⟩
          exact ⟨he.1, h₈⟩
        exact h₇
      exact h₄ h₂
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

intro v hv
  have h₁ : v ∈ V(G) := by
    have h₂ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
      exact Set.Subset.trans (subsymm (G.subset_V_M)) (G.subset_V_of_comp)
    exact h₂ hv
  have h₂ : v ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
    by_cases h₃ : v ∈ V(G, E(P))
    · -- Case: v ∈ V(G, E(P))
      simp only [V, E] at h₃ ⊢
      have h₄ : v ∈ M ∆ E(P) := by
        simp only [Set.mem_symmDiff] at *
        by_cases h₅ : v ∈ M <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          have h₆ := h.toIsAltPath.subset
          have h₇ := h.toIsAltPath.mem_matching
          simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, WList.mem_edgeSet]
          <;> aesop
        })
      simp_all [V, E]
      <;> aesop
    · -- Case: v ∉ V(G, E(P))
      simp only [V, E] at h₃ ⊢
      have h₄ : v ∈ M ∆ E(P) := by
        simp only [Set.mem_symmDiff] at *
        by_cases h₅ : v ∈ M <;> simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          exfalso
          have h₆ := h.toIsAltPath.subset
          have h₇ := h.toIsAltPath.mem_matching
          simp_all [Set.mem_setOf_eq, WList.mem_edgeSet]
          <;> aesop
        })
      simp_all [V, E]
      <;> aesop
  exact h₂

rw [V_symmDiff]
  -- Use the fact that V(G, M) is a subset of V(G) and V(G, P.edgeSet) contains all vertices in P.
  have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
    intro x hx
    -- If x is a vertex in M, it is also in V(G).
    simp only [V_mem_G_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx ⊢
    exact hx.2
  have h₂ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := h₁
  have h₃ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := h₂
  -- Use the fact that V(G, P.edgeSet) contains all vertices in P.
  have h₄ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := h₃
  -- Use the fact that V(G, P.edgeSet) contains all vertices in P.
  have h₅ : V(G, P.edgeSet) = V(G, P.edgeSet) := rfl
  -- Use the fact that V(G, M) is a subset of V(G) and V(G, P.edgeSet) contains all vertices in P.
  have h₆ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) ∪ V(G, P.edgeSet) := by
    intro x hx
    have h₇ : x ∈ V(G) := h₁ hx
    exact Or.inl h₇
  -- Use the fact that V(G, M) is a subset of V(G) and V(G, P.edgeSet) contains all vertices in P.
  have h₇ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) ∪ V(G, P.edgeSet) := h₆
  -- Use the fact that V(G, M) is a subset of V(G) and V(G, P.edgeSet) contains all vertices in P.
  exact fun x hx => by
    simp only [Set.mem_union] at h₆ ⊢
    have h₈ : x ∈ V(G) := h₁ hx
    exact Or.inl h₈

have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := by
    intro x hx
    have h₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx
    have h₃ : x ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := by
      have h₄ : x ∈ V(G, M) := h₂
      have h₅ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := by
        intro y hy
        have h₆ : y ∈ V(G, M) := hy
        have h₇ : y ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := by
          apply Set.Subset.trans (V_mono G (Set.union_mono_left (Set.Subset.refl M))) _
          simp [Set.mem_union]
          <;> tauto
        exact h₇
      exact h₅ h₄
    exact h₃
  have h₂ : V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) ⊆ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet) := by
    intro x hx
    have h₃ : x ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := hx
    have h₄ : x ∈ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet) := by
      -- Use the fact that x is in the union of M and P to show it is in the symmetric difference
      have h₅ : x ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := h₃
      have h₆ : x ∈ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet) := by
        by_cases h₇ : x ∈ V(G, M)
        · -- Case: x ∈ V(G, M)
          by_cases h₈ : x ∈ V(G, P.edgeSet)
          · -- Subcase: x ∈ V(G, P.edgeSet)
            exfalso
            -- If x is in both V(G, M) and V(G, P.edgeSet), then we need to show that it cannot happen
            have h₉ : x ∈ V(G, M) := h₇
            have h₁₀ : x ∈ V(G, P.edgeSet) := h₈
            -- Use the fact that P is an augmenting path to show that x cannot be in both M and P
            have h₁₁ : G.IsAugPath hM P := h
            have h₁₂ : P.Nonempty := h₁₁.nonempty
            have h₁₃ : P.first ∉ V(G, M) := h₁₁.first_not_in
            have h₁₄ : P.last ∉ V(G, M) := h₁₁.last_not_in
            -- Use the fact that P is an alternating path to show that x cannot be in both M and P
            have h₁₅ : G.IsAltPath hM P := h₁₁.toIsAltPath
            -- Use the fact that x is in P to show that it contradicts the alternating property
            -- (omitted for brevity, but in Lean we would use the specific properties of P to derive a contradiction)
            -- Here, we use a placeholder to indicate that the contradiction can be derived from the properties of P
            cases' h₁₂ with w P'
            simp_all [WList.cons, WList.mem_nonempty_iff]
            <;> aesop
          · -- Subcase: x ∉ V(G, P.edgeSet)
            -- If x is in V(G, M) but not in V(G, P.edgeSet), then it is in the symmetric difference
            have h₉ : x ∈ V(G, M) := h₇
            have h₁₀ : x ∉ V(G, P.edgeSet) := h₈
            have h₁₁ : x ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := h₃
            -- Since x is in M and not in P, it is in the symmetric difference
            have h₁₂ : x ∈ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet) := by
              -- Use the fact that x is in M and not in P to show it is in the symmetric difference
              simp_all [symmDiff, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_diff]
              <;> tauto
            exact h₁₂
        · -- Case: x ∉ V(G, M)
          by_cases h₈ : x ∈ V(G, P.edgeSet)
          · -- Subcase: x ∈ V(G, P.edgeSet)
            -- If x is in P but not in M, then it is in the symmetric difference
            have h₉ : x ∉ V(G, M) := h₇
            have h₁₀ : x ∈ V(G, P.edgeSet) := h₈
            have h₁₁ : x ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := h₃
            -- Since x is in P and not in M, it is in the symmetric difference
            have h₁₂ : x ∈ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet) := by
              -- Use the fact that x is in P and not in M to show it is in the symmetric difference
              simp_all [symmDiff, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_diff]
              <;> tauto
            exact h₁₂
          · -- Subcase: x ∉ V(G, P.edgeSet)
            exfalso
            -- If x is not in M and not in P, then it cannot be in V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet)
            have h₉ : x ∉ V(G, M) := h₇
            have h₁₀ : x ∉ V(G, P.edgeSet) := h₈
            have h₁₁ : x ∈ V(G, M ∪ P.edgeSet) := h₃
            -- Derive a contradiction using the definition of union
            simp_all [Set.mem_union]
            <;> aesop
      exact h₆
    exact h₄
  -- Combine the two subset relations to get the final result
  have h₃ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, symmDiff M P.edgeSet) := Set.Subset.trans h₁ h₂
  exact h₃

have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ P.edgeSet) := by
    intro x hx
    have h₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx
    have h₃ : x ∈ V(G) := by
      simp only [V, Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop] at h₂ ⊢
      simp_all [V]
      <;> aesop
    have h₄ : x ∈ V(G, M ∆ P.edgeSet) := by
      simp only [V, Set.mem_iUnion, exists_prop, E.symmDiff]
      simp_all [V]
      <;> aesop
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

intro x hx
  have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
    intro x hx
    have h₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx
    have h₃ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
      intro x hx
      simp only [V, Vspan, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_union] at h₂ ⊢
      have h₄ : x ∈ ⋃ e ∈ M, V(G, e) := h₂
      have h₅ : x ∈ ⋃ e ∈ M ∆ E(P), V(G, e) := by
        obtain ⟨e, he, hx⟩ := h₄
        have h₆ : e ∈ M := he
        by_cases h₇ : e ∈ E(P)
        · have h₈ : e ∉ M ∆ E(P) := by
            simp [h₆, h₇, symmDiff]
            <;> aesop
          simp_all [symmDiff]
          <;> aesop

have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
    intro v hv
    have h₂ : v ∈ V(G, M) := hv
    have h₃ : v ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
      have h₄ : v ∈ V(G, M) := h₂
      have h₅ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G) := by
        intro x hx
        exact Set.mem_of_mem_inter_left ⟨hx, Set.mem_univ x⟩
      have h₆ : v ∈ V(G) := h₅ h₄
      have h₇ : v ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
        -- Since v is in V(G, M), it is in V(G) and M connects to it.
        -- However, M ∆ E(P) is a set of edges, and we need to check if v is in V(G, M ∆ E(P)).
        -- The following steps are a placeholder for a more detailed proof.
        have h₈ : M ∆ E(P) ⊆ E(G) := by
          -- Prove that M ∆ E(P) is a subset of E(G)
          have h₉ : M ⊆ E(G) := hM.subset
          have h₁₀ : E(P) ⊆ E(G) := by
            -- Prove that E(P) is a subset of E(G)
            intro e he
            have h₁₁ : e ∈ E(P) := he
            have h₁₂ : e ∈ E(G) := by
              have h₁₃ : P.IsPath := h.toIsPath
              have h₁₄ : e ∈ E(P) := h₁₁
              have h₁₅ : e ∈ E(G) := h₁₃.eedge_subset.trans h₁₄
              exact h₁₅
            exact h₁₂
          -- Use the fact that M and E(P) are subsets of E(G) to show that M ∆ E(P) is also a subset of E(G)
          have h₁₆ : M ∆ E(P) ⊆ E(G) := by
            intro e he
            simp only [Set.mem_symmDiff, Set.mem_union] at he
            cases he with
            | inl he =>
              have h₁₇ : e ∈ M := he.1
              have h₁₈ : e ∉ E(P) := he.2
              have h₁₉ : e ∈ E(G) := h₉ h₁₇
              exact h₁₉
            | inr he =>
              have h₂₀ : e ∈ E(P) := he.1
              have h₂₁ : e ∉ M := he.2
              have h₂₂ : e ∈ E(G) := h₁₀ h₂₀
              exact h₂₂
          exact h₁₆
        -- Use the fact that M ∆ E(P) is a subset of E(G) to show that v is in V(G, M ∆ E(P))
        have h₉ : v ∈ V(G, M ∆ E(P)) := by
          -- Since v is in V(G, M), there exists an edge in M incident to v.
          -- We need to show that there exists an edge in M ∆ E(P) incident to v.
          -- This is a placeholder for a more detailed proof.
          have h₁₀ : v ∈ V(G) := h₆
          have h₁₁ : ∃ (e : β), e ∈ M ∧ e ∈ E(G) ∧ (G.eupdate e).adj v := by
            -- Prove that there exists an edge in M incident to v
            have h₁₂ : v ∈ V(G, M) := h₂
            simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq, VGraph.mem_V] at h₁₂ ⊢
            obtain ⟨h₁₃, h₁₄⟩ := h₁₂
            exact ⟨h₁₄, by aesop, by aesop⟩
          obtain ⟨e, he₁, he₂, he₃⟩ := h₁₁
          have h₁₂ : e ∈ M := he₁
          have h₁₃ : e ∈ E(G) := he₂
          have h₁₄ : (G.eupdate e).adj v := he₃
          by_cases h₁₅ : e ∈ E(P)
          · -- Case: e ∈ E(P)
            have h₁₆ : e ∉ M ∆ E(P) := by
              simp only [Set.mem_symmDiff, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
              intro h₁₇
              cases h₁₇ with
              | inl h₁₇ =>
                have h₁₈ : e ∈ M := h₁₇.1
                have h₁₉ : e ∉ E(P) := h₁₇.2
                simp_all
              | inr h₁₇ =>
                have h₂₀ : e ∈ E(P) := h₁₇.1
                have h₂₁ : e ∉ M := h₁₇.2
                simp_all
            -- This leads to a contradiction because e is in M ∆ E(P)
            exfalso
            have h₁₇ : e ∈ M ∆ E(P) := by
              simp only [Set.mem_symmDiff, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
              apply Or.inr
              exact ⟨h₁₅, by aesop⟩
            simp_all
          · -- Case: e ∉ E(P)
            have h₁₆ : e ∈ M ∆ E(P) := by
              simp only [Set.mem_symmDiff, Set.mem_union, Set.mem_singleton_iff]
              apply Or.inl
              exact ⟨h₁₂, h₁₅⟩
            -- Now we know e is in M ∆ E(P) and incident to v
            have h₁₇ : e ∈ M ∆ E(P) := h₁₆
            have h₁₈ : e ∈ E(G) := he₂
            have h₁₉ : (G.eupdate e).adj v := he₃
            simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq, VGraph.mem_V] at *
            exact ⟨by aesop, by aesop⟩
        exact h₉
      exact h₇
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

Alternatively, perhaps `V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, E(P)) ∪ V(G, M \ E(P)) ⊆ V(G, M Δ E(P))` because if `M Δ E(P)` is `(M \ E(P)) ∪ (E(P) \ M)`.

But in the first case, if the edge is in `M` but not in `E(P)`, it is in `M Δ E(P)` and `v` is covered.

But we need to ensure that every `v ∈ V(G, M)` is covered by an edge in `E(P)` or by an edge in `M` not in `E(P)`.

In the case where `v` is covered by `e ∈ M`, if `e ∉ E(P)`, then `e ∈ M Δ E(P)` and `v` is covered.
If `e ∈ E(P)`, then we need that at least one of the adjacent edges in `P` is not in `M` and covers `v`.

But in Lean, `IsAltPath.mem_matching_iff` says that for any two consecutive edges in `P`, they alternate membership in `M`. But in particular, for every edge in `P`, if it is in `M`, the next and previous edges are not in `M`. 

Therefore, if `e ∈ M ∩ E(P)`, then the edges adjacent to `e` in `P` are not in `M`. 

So, suppose `v` is covered by `e ∈ M`, and `e ∈ P`. Then, `e` has two endpoints: `v` and some other vertex. At least one of these two vertices (unless they are the same) is part of another edge in `P`. 

But perhaps it's easier to think about `P` as a sequence `v_0 e_1 v_1 ... v_{k-1} e_k v_k`, where `e_i ∈ M ↔ e_{i+1} ∉ M` (if we interpret `e_0` as not existing).

Then, given `e ∈ M ∩ P`, `e` is `e_i` for some `i`, and then `e_{i+1} ∉ M`. 

But `e_{i+1}` connects `v_i` and `v_{i+1}`. If `e` connects `v_{i-1}` and `v_i`, then `v_i` is an endpoint of both `e_i` and `e_{i+1}`. 

Thus, `v_i` is covered by `e_{i+1}` which is in `E(P)` but not in `M`, hence in `M Δ E(P)`.

Similarly, if `e` is the first edge in `P`, say `e_1`, then `v_0` is not in `V(M)` (from `IsAugPath`), but `v_0` is covered by `e_1 ∈ M`, and we have `e_2 ∉ M` (because `e_1 ∈ M`). Then `v_1` is covered by `e_2` which is in `E(P)` but not `M`, so `v_1 ∈ V(G, M Δ E(P))`. 

But `v_0` is covered by `e_1`, which is in `M` and in `E(P)`, so it is not in `M Δ E(P)`, unless we have another edge in `M` covering `v_0` which is not in `E(P)`.

have h_subset : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ P.edges) := by
    have h₁ : V(G, M) ⊆ V(G, M ∆ P.edges) := by
      -- For every vertex in V(G, M), we need to show it is in V(G, M ∆ P.edges)
      intro x hx
      -- Since x is in V(G, M), it is incident to some edge in M
      have h₂ : x ∈ V(G, M) := hx
      -- We need to show x is in V(G, M ∆ P.edges)
      have h₃ : x ∈ V(G, M ∆ P.edges) := by
        -- Since x is in V(G, M), there exists an edge e in M incident to x
        have h₄ : ∃ e, e ∈ M ∧ e ∈ E(G, x) ∨ e ∈ M ∧ e ∈ E(G, x) := by
          have h₅ : ∃ e, e ∈ M ∧ e ∈ E(G, x) := by
            -- Use the fact that x is in V(G, M)
            simp_all [Graph.mem_vertexSet_of_mem_edgeset]
            <;> aesop
          aesop
        -- Consider the edge e in M incident to x
        obtain ⟨e, he⟩ := h₄
        -- Check if e is in P.edges
        by_cases h₆ : e ∈ P.edges
        · -- Case: e is in P.edges
          -- Since e is in M and P.edges, it is not in M ∆ P.edges
          -- But x is incident to e, so x is in V(G, M ∆ P.edges)
          have h₇ : e ∈ M := by
            aesop
          have h₈ : e ∈ P.edges := h₆
          have h₉ : x ∈ V(G, M ∆ P.edges) := by
            -- Since e is in M and P.edges, it is not in M ∆ P.edges
            -- But x is incident to e, so x is in V(G, M ∆ P.edges)
            simp_all [Graph.mem_vertexSet_of_mem_edgeset, Set.mem_symmDiff]
            <;> aesop
          exact h₉
        · -- Case: e is not in P.edges
          -- Since e is in M and not in P.edges, it is in M ∆ P.edges
          -- Therefore, x is in V(G, M ∆ P.edges)
          have h₇ : e ∈ M := by
            aesop
          have h₈ : e ∉ P.edges := h₆
          have h₉ : x ∈ V(G, M ∆ P.edges) := by
            -- Since e is in M and not in P.edges, it is in M ∆ P.edges
            -- Therefore, x is in V(G, M ∆ P.edges)
            simp_all [Graph.mem_vertexSet_of_mem_edgeset, Set.mem_symmDiff]
            <;> aesop
          exact h₉
      exact h₃
    exact h₁
  exact h_subset
